name: The Goblin Menace
description: Marcus needs help clearing out the goblins infesting the dungeon.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: Those blasted goblins have been raiding my supply carts! I can't keep my shop stocked at this rate. Could you clear out at least three of them?

responses.text: I'll take care of those goblins for you.
Maybe you should learn to fight them yourself.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: Excellent! Slay three of those little thieves and I'll make it worth your while. I've got a fine steel dagger set aside for you.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: I understand, adventurer. The offer stands if you change your mind.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: Ha! I'm a merchant, not a warrior. But I know how to reward those who are.

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: Still working on those goblins? Keep at it! The dungeon is crawling with the little pests.

responses.text: How many more do I need?
These goblins are tougher than expected.

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: Good luck out there!

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: Check your quest tracker - it'll tell you how many remain.

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: They're quick, but they're fragile. Watch for their charge attacks.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: You've done it! I can already see fewer goblins lurking about. Here's your reward, as promised!

responses.text: Glad I could help. I'll take that reward.
Keep the reward, I did it for the thrill.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: Here you are - gold and a fine steel dagger! My shipments should be safer now.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: Straight to business, eh? I like that. Here's everything I promised.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: Nonsense! A deal's a deal. Take the reward - you've earned it.

completed.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
completed.text: Thanks again for dealing with those goblins. My supply lines are running smoothly now!

completed.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
completed.text: You're a true hero. Don't forget to check my shop for new stock!

completed.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
completed.text: Not at the moment, but keep checking back!

name: The Lost Shipment
description: Aldric needs health potions recovered from the dungeon to continue his research.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: My last shipment of health potions was lost in the dungeon. I need at least three for my research. Could you gather them for me? Monsters sometimes carry them.

responses.text: I'll find those potions for you.
Why can't you get them yourself, old man?

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: Wonderful! Bring me three health potions and I'll reward you with some of my finest large potions. They're far more potent.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: The spirits are patient, and so am I. Return when you're ready.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: Ha! These old bones aren't suited for dungeon crawling anymore. But I make up for it with wisdom... and generous rewards.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: Have you found the potions yet? Monsters in the dungeon sometimes drop them when defeated.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: Keep searching. The dungeon will provide.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: Monsters often carry potions. Defeat enough of them and you're sure to find some.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: Patience is the companion of wisdom, young one. You'll find them.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: Ah, you have the potions! I can sense their essence from here. Hand them over and I'll give you your reward.

responses.text: These better be worth the trouble.
Do you really need all three?

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: Perfect! And as promised, here are two large health potions. They'll serve you much better in the deeper chambers.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: Oh, they are! These large potions heal twice as much. A fair trade, I'd say.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: I'm afraid so - the arcane formula requires exactly three. But these large potions will more than compensate you!

completed.speaker: Aldric the Sage
completed.text: My research progresses well, thanks to those potions. You have my gratitude.

responses.text: Glad I could help with your research.

completed.speaker: Aldric the Sage
completed.text: The ancient spirits smile upon you, adventurer.

completed.speaker: Aldric the Sage
completed.text: Not at the moment, but the spirits may guide me to ask again.

name: Orc Persecution
description: Elena is being threatened by orc warriors. She needs someone to thin their ranks.

offer.speaker: Elena the Exotic
offer.text: Those brute orc warriors have been threatening my trade routes. I need at least two of them dealt with before I can safely import more rare goods. Interested?

responses.text: Consider it done. I'll handle the orcs.
Sounds like a problem you should solve with gold, not swords.

offer.speaker: Elena the Exotic
offer.text: Wonderful! Kill two orc warriors and I'll reward you with a silver sword from my personal collection. It's worth more than its weight in gold.

offer.speaker: Elena the Exotic
offer.text: I understand. Orcs are fearsome foes. The offer remains open when you're ready.

offer.speaker: Elena the Exotic
offer.text: You can't bribe an orc, darling. But you can certainly be rewarded for defeating one.

inProgress.speaker: Elena the Exotic
inProgress.text: How goes the orc hunting? Those brutes tend to lurk in the deeper rooms.

inProgress.speaker: Elena the Exotic
inProgress.text: Excellent. I look forward to hearing of your victory.

inProgress.speaker: Elena the Exotic
inProgress.text: They're slow but strong. Keep your distance and strike when they're recovering from an attack.

inProgress.speaker: Elena the Exotic
inProgress.text: Indeed they do. Make sure you have potions ready. The reward will be worth the bruises.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Elena the Exotic
readyToTurnIn.text: I can tell by that victorious look - the orcs have been dealt with! Time for your reward.

responses.text: The orcs won't bother you anymore.
That silver sword - hand it over.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Elena the Exotic
readyToTurnIn.text: You have my deepest thanks! Here - a silver sword and a pouch of gold. You've earned every coin.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Elena the Exotic
readyToTurnIn.text: Direct! I like that. Here it is - a fine silver blade, plus gold for your trouble.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Elena the Exotic
readyToTurnIn.text: I'm sure it was! Here's your reward. That silver sword will serve you well in future battles.

completed.speaker: Elena the Exotic
completed.text: Trade is flowing smoothly again, thanks to you. My inventory has never been better!

completed.speaker: Elena the Exotic
completed.text: Come browse my wares anytime, darling. You've earned a loyal customer discount... in spirit.

completed.speaker: Elena the Exotic
completed.text: Nothing right now, but danger always finds a way back. Check in later.

name: The Sage's Apprentice
description: Aldric's apprentice was overwhelmed by skeletons deep in the dungeon. Clear the undead to make the path safe.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: My apprentice ventured too deep and barely escaped a pack of skeletons. The path must be cleared before they can return safely. Will you slay four skeletons for me?

responses.text: I have other priorities right now.
Your apprentice should learn to fight.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: Bless you! Destroy four skeletons and I'll prepare my finest healing potions as payment. Three large ones should keep you alive in the deepest depths.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: I understand. My apprentice can wait a while longer. Return when you're able.

offer.speaker: Aldric the Sage
offer.text: They're a scholar, not a soldier. Not everyone solves problems with a blade... though right now, that's exactly what's needed.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: The skeletons still roam. My apprentice grows anxious. How goes the hunt?

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: The spirits watch over you. Be careful.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: Skeletons haunt the middle chambers. They're quick but brittle - a strong hit will shatter them.

inProgress.speaker: Aldric the Sage
inProgress.text: Fast but fragile. Time your attacks well and they'll crumble.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: The arcane winds tell me the skeletal threat has diminished. You've succeeded!

responses.text: The path is clear for your apprentice.
Those skeletons won't bother anyone now.
Your 'arcane winds' could've mentioned how many there were.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: My apprentice can now return safely. Here - three large healing potions, brewed with extra care!

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: Indeed! Here's your well-earned reward. These potions will serve you well.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Aldric the Sage
readyToTurnIn.text: Ha! The winds are vague about details. But they're very clear about rewarding heroes. Take these potions!

completed.speaker: Aldric the Sage
completed.text: My apprentice has returned safely and resumed their studies. You have my eternal gratitude.

completed.speaker: Aldric the Sage
completed.text: May the ancient spirits guide your blade, brave one.

completed.speaker: Aldric the Sage
completed.text: All is peaceful for now. But the dungeon is never truly quiet for long.

name: The Undead Uprising
description: Marcus has heard reports of zombies appearing in unusual numbers. Investigate and eliminate the threat.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: Something foul is stirring in the dungeon. Zombies are appearing in numbers I've never seen before. I need someone to investigate - kill three zombies and bring back two health potions you find as evidence of the corruption.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: Be careful out there! Kill three zombies and recover two health potions from the corrupted areas. I'll reward you with a war hammer - perfect for crushing the undead.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: I don't blame you. But someone needs to look into this before it gets worse.

offer.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
offer.text: Dramatic? I've lost three delivery runners this week! When you see the horde yourself, you'll understand.

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: Any progress on the undead situation? Remember, I need the zombies dealt with AND those potion samples.

responses.text: Why do you need the potions?

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: Good. Stay vigilant - zombies are slow but relentless.

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: The potions found near zombie nests may be tainted. I need to examine them to understand what's causing this uprising.

inProgress.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
inProgress.text: That confirms my fears. Clear what you can and bring those samples back.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: You look like you've been through quite the ordeal! Do you have the zombie kills and the potion evidence?

responses.text: That was more zombies than you mentioned...

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: Excellent work! These potion samples will help me understand the corruption. Here's the war hammer I promised - it'll crush undead bones like twigs!

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: Right here! A fine war hammer, perfect for your next encounter with the undead. Gold too, of course.

readyToTurnIn.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
readyToTurnIn.text: I feared as much. The situation is worse than I thought. Take this hammer and gold - you'll need them for what's to come.

completed.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
completed.text: I've analyzed the potions you brought back. There's definitely dark magic at work, but the immediate threat has passed.

responses.text: Dark magic? Should I be worried?

completed.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
completed.text: More than you know. Stay safe out there, friend.

completed.speaker: Marcus the Merchant
completed.text: Perhaps... but that's a problem for another day. For now, the dungeon is safer thanks to you.
